page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the legacy N1 machine family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs, mapped from 2 physical Intel Xeon cores running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 0.9 GB per vCPU. It does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations place the n1-highcpu-4 in the lower performance tiers. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests register in the bottom 25%, with scores of 1125.48 and 2941.66 respectively. Memory performance is similarly constrained, with uncached large-block read bandwidth at 25.58 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 21.69 GB/sec. Cryptographic and compression tests, such as single-threaded gzip compression at 18.99 GB/sec, also fall into the bottom 10% to 25% of tested hardware.

Given its low memory allocation of 1 GB per vCPU and older Intel Xeon architecture, this server is designed for compute-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints. It is suitable for lightweight batch processing, basic web serving, and simple utility scripting. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n1-highcpu-4 offers a low-cost entry point for dedicated compute resources, though it presents a clear tradeoff in raw processing speed and memory capacity compared to newer generation instances.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 architecture server from the N1 family, configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz, this instance features a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 0.9 GB per core, with no bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate modest performance, with stress-ng CPU and memory bandwidth metrics placing the server in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. It achieves a static web serving throughput of 2.92 GB/sec and a Redis SET performance of 2.8 million operations per second. This server is best suited for lightweight, compute-focused workloads with minimal memory requirements, such as basic web serving, simple batch processing, and utility scripting, offering a budget-friendly option with dedicated CPU resources at the expense of modern processing speeds.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 server with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM. It is designed for lightweight, compute-focused workloads requiring minimal memory capacity.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-4 features 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM, offering a budget-friendly option for low-memory compute tasks.
tagline: A budget-friendly, low-memory compute instance featuring four dedicated vCPUs for lightweight processing and basic web serving tasks.

4.0 GB of system memory with 0.9 GB per vCPU
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Static web serving throughput of 2.92 GB/sec
Best suited for lightweight batch processing and basic web serving
